subject: Project Management
title: The First Factories
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The First Factories.

hook.text: Before factories, every product was handmade. The Industrial Revolution changed everything.

buildup.text: Arkwright's cotton mills in the 1770s ran on water power, shifts, and rigid schedules.

discovery.text: For the first time, work was broken into repeatable steps performed by different people.

twist.text: Workers hated it. Luddites smashed machines. But productivity soared over 1,000%.

climax.text: The factory model became the template: divide, specialize, and coordinate.

punchline.text: The first project framework was a factory floor.

quiz.question: What was revolutionary about the factory model for project management?
options: Work was divided into repeatable steps done by different people
Factories eliminated the need for managers
